task_completion.description: Verify that the patient record for John Zenon is opened in GNU Health.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNU Health interface should display the patient record or dashboard specifically for 'John Zenon'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a clinical evaluation is performed and documents the patient's fever.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clinical evaluation, encounter, or consultation form for John Zenon should show 'fever' or 'undulating fever' documented in the symptoms, signs, or clinical notes section.

task_completion.description: Verify that the patient is formally diagnosed with Brucellosis using the correct ICD-10 code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The diagnosis or conditions list for John Zenon should include an entry for Brucellosis with an ICD-10 code starting with 'A23'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the standard dual-antibiotic therapy is prescribed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The medication or prescription section for John Zenon should list two antibiotics: Doxycycline and Rifampicin (Tetracycline and Streptomycin are also acceptable alternatives).

task_completion.description: Verify that at least three baseline laboratory tests are ordered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The lab orders or requests section for John Zenon should display a list of at least three distinct laboratory tests.

task_completion.description: Verify that a test-of-cure follow-up appointment is scheduled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The appointments or scheduling section should show a future follow-up appointment created for John Zenon (ideally 28 to 45 days after the initial visit).

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health desktop client being used, with standard windows, menus, and forms visible.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open showing SQL queries, Python scripts, or direct database manipulations to insert the patient data.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent modified the actual patient record rather than typing answers into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data entry must occur within the GNU Health application fields (e.g., diagnosis, prescriptions, lab orders) and not in a separate notepad or text application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is 'John Zenon'. The ICD-10 code for Brucellosis must start with 'A23'. The required dual-antibiotic therapy is Doxycycline and Rifampicin (Tetracycline and Streptomycin are acceptable alternatives). At least 3 laboratory tests must be ordered. The follow-up appointment should be scheduled between 28 and 45 days from the current date.
